author: potuz
content: we do default this as well, min bids make more difference though, this whole flag is useless anyway and should probably dissapear

author: nflaig
content: we still need a lever for validator to signal local only to the beacon node, this could be a boolean flag, but to me it seems removing `builder_boost_factor` is more effort than actually keeping it, and would be a breaking change from v3 to v4, which is fine as we bump the version, just not sure if it's worth the change, so the status quo to me seems to just keep `builder_boost_factor` unless people pushing for removal

author: potuz
content: Min bid of infty is good for this

author: nflaig
content: but so you are saying we pass `min_bid` from the validator client via query param to the beacon?

author: tersec
content: is true local-only and no builder that important to have? the existing local-if-possible definitely has value

author: tersec
content: but if the local building fails, I don't really see the point in requiring the node not to use an external bid

author: potuz
content: the boost factor flag is used to compare an external block vs the local block. The min bid has the same effect: compare an external bid with a local block. The latter is more effective at filtering than the former. I haven' t produced a builder' s block ever

author: nflaig
content: ah no I meant local preferred, the current v3 spec doesn't express local only either, and it does not make much sense

author: tersec
content: right, ok

author: potuz
content: And yes, more future proof, I believe we should all change our views on external building. ePBS brings the whole P2P stack to be that. Local building should not be viewed as superior or less censoring, it should only be viewed as an extreme fallback in case of global instability

author: nflaig
content: why not less censoring, I don't see hwo builder blocks change with gloas

author: nflaig
content: unless we have focil, then it changes

author: potuz
content: we really want to be in a place in which you do not need a beefy node to be a validator, but you do need a beefy node to propose a good block. Attesters and proposers do not need the same level of decentralization

author: tersec
content: in isolation I'd call the `builder_boost_factor` a failed experiment and with hindsight, it wouldn't be something I'd replicate, but it might indeed be easier to keep it -- it does work, we have all implemented it, and it has broadly the functionality we need

author: potuz
content: because home stakers with overdetermined nodes will send P2P bids equivalently to the current vanilla self-built blocks by default. Eg my own node will do this.

author: potuz
content: you just need 1 of N honest to provide this

author: nflaig
content: yes but a proposer will never select that bid

author: nflaig
content: it will select titans bid which might be censoring

author: potuz
content: well, not with min-bid

author: tersec
content: this is going to happen necessarily with the zkp-based part of the roadmap

author: tersec
content: $100k provers

author: nflaig
content: but min-bid contradicts what you said here https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1509586658096447619/1509908685856702626 ?

author: nflaig
content: why would you use min-bid if " should only be viewed as an extreme fallback in case of global instability"

author: nflaig
content: that seems you imply only local build if circuit breaker is triggered

author: potuz
content: min bid could be for the trusted calls

author: potuz
content: Titan is not sending bids over P2P

author: potuz
content: and yes, the whole system is gameable pre-FOCIL by some censoring node sending bids over P2P

author: potuz
content: which again is why I wanted a very simple boolean implemented right now that returns censoring/not-censoring on the call to newPayload

author: potuz
content: that already is good enough to fall back to local building

author: nflaig
content: but this doesn't work if titan has 100x builders onchain?

author: nflaig
content: also I don't think this is needed with FOCIL which we have soon and more effective

author: tersec
content: FOCIL will come in Hegotá and self-building will be just as feasible in a Glamsterdam timeframe as Fusaka

author: tersec
content: this seems like more instantly obsolete jank of the sort Ethereum likes to codify

author: tersec
content: Glamsterdam doesn't actually create a regression in this regards, no new/sudden urgency

author: nflaig
content: but also not an improvement, at least I don't see how, builders can still censor as today if they want, there is stuff like `shouldOverrideBuilder` on the engine api, and min-bid globally can be effective, but I don't see anything that's bullet proof until FOCIL

author: tersec
content: Yeah, it's status quo until FOCIL

author: nflaig
content: echoing this, I wouldn't introduce this but it doesn't hurt either and is implemented already, and I wanna avoid more api churn for gloas, it's already enough as is
